challenges.title: Basic Column Layout
challenges.description: Write CSS that uses CSS columns to create a multi-column text layout. Create a rule for `.columns` that sets `column-count: 3` and `column-gap: 30px`. Assign your CSS to the variable `css`.
challenges.test_code:
assert 'column-count' in css.lower(), 'Should use column-count property'
assert 'column-gap' in css.lower(), 'Should use column-gap property'
assert '3' in css, 'Should set column-count to 3'
assert '.columns' in css.lower(), 'Should target .columns'
hints: column-count specifies how many columns the content should be divided into
column-gap controls the space between columns

challenges.title: Column Rules and Spanning
challenges.description: Write CSS for a styled column layout. Create a rule for `.article` that sets `column-count: 2`, `column-gap: 40px`, and `column-rule: 1px solid #ccc` to add a visible divider between columns. Create a rule for `.article h2` that sets `column-span: all` so headings stretch across all columns. Assign your CSS to the variable `css`.
challenges.test_code:
assert 'column-count' in css.lower(), 'Should use column-count'
assert 'column-rule' in css.lower(), 'Should use column-rule for a divider between columns'
assert 'column-span' in css.lower(), 'Should use column-span'
assert 'all' in css.lower(), 'Should set column-span to all'
assert '.article' in css.lower(), 'Should target .article'
assert 'h2' in css.lower(), 'Should target h2 within .article'
hints: column-rule works like the border shorthand: width, style, and color
column-span: all makes an element stretch across all columns
